title: Argentina sumatrana
intro: Argentina sumatrana, also known as Argentine Suma or Suma, is a type of plant that belongs to the Malpighiaceae family and is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost energy levels. The bioactive constituents of Argentina sumatrana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Argentina sumatrana is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into supplements and dietary supplements, particularly in traditional medicine and Latin American herbalism.
taxonomy: Argentina sumatrana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of South America, specifically in the countries of Peru and possibly Brazil and Colombia.
